title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
description: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

title: Oil jumps back above $70 as fresh U.S.-Iran clashes reignite Middle East supply fears
description: Oil edged higher on Monday after renewed military strikes between the U.S. and Iran reignited concerns over crude supplies from the Middle East.

title: Baidu shares jump 7% as AI chip arm Kunlunxin said to target $50 billion Hong Kong IPO
description: Baidu's Hong Kong-listed shares rose over 6% amid reports that its AI chip unit Kunlunxin is targeting a $50 billion initial public offering in the city.

title: Samsung, SK Hynix shares fall as investors brace for reported $1.3 trillion spending plans
description: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix plunged after reports surfaced that the pair are expected to unveil investment plans worth hundreds of billions of dollars.

title: Oil prices, stock futures inch higher as U.S. and Iran reportedly agree to halt attacks
description: Oil prices rose Sunday and U.S. stock-index futures advanced, after the U.S. and Iran reportedly agreed to halt attacks after repeatedly exchanging fire in the Persian Gulf over the weekend.

title: Europe risks starting winter with gas stocks at 15-year low
description: Storage facilities in the EU are not being refilled fast enough ahead of colder months

title: Virgin Media O2 bonds slide as broadband rivals squeeze cash flow
description: Telecoms group’s bonds tumble as altnets take market share and investors fret over £2bn Netomnia deal

title: The new AI-based world order
description: A single factor is now dictating the hierarchy of global returns

title: The leap of faith behind SpaceX’s mega bond deal
description: The views of equity and bond investors might one day be at odds with each other

title: Japan IPOs fall to 15-year low with no rapid rebound in sight
description: Tokyo’s surging stocks fail to jump-start lacklustre listings market in first half of this year

title: PBOC’s New Overnight Rate Said Below Forecasts in Hint at Easing
description: China’s central bank set the interest rate on its new overnight liquidity tool at a level that was below expectations, according to people familiar with the matter, in what some economists see as a de facto rate cut that could push down market borrowing costs.

title: Markets Are Looking Past the Ceasefire. Then What?
description: Rates and currency volatility could dominate the second half of the year.

title: GM-Backed Self-Driving Firm Momenta Seeks $752 Million From Hong Kong Listing
description: Autonomous-driving firm Momenta Global Ltd. started taking investor orders for its Hong Kong initial public offering to raise HK$5.9 billion ($752 million), pouncing on a multiyear high for share sales in the city.

title: Markets Are Buying Into Less Uncertainty: Capital.com
description: Kyle Rodda of Capital.com shares his views on the de-dollarization trade and market volatility. He says it shouldn't be understated "how significant it is that the United States is inviting Iran, effectively, back into global trade, selling its oil into global markets." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Copper Declines as Fed Stance and Strong Dollar Weigh On Metals
description: Copper fell as the Federal Reserve’s hawkish stance and a rising US dollar continued to weigh on sentiment toward industrial metals.

title: Indian Rupee Leads Asian Peers on Falling Crude, Capital Push
description: The RBI may need to let the rupee rise further before resuming dollars purchases.

title: Russia Expands LNG Dark Fleet Effort With a 19-Year-Old Tanker
description: A liquefied natural gas tanker docked at a US-sanctioned storage unit in Russia’s Murmansk region, the first time the vessel has loaded blacklisted fuel and the latest sign of Moscow’s efforts to expand exports despite Western sanctions.

title: China Says Tech Growth a Challenge to Predicting Energy Demand
description: China faces greater uncertainty in forecasting energy demand as structural changes in the economy and the rapid expansion of new industries reshape consumption patterns, according to a top government official.

title: Pakistan Says Strikes Along Afghan Border Kill 29 Militants
description: Pakistan said it carried out an intelligence-based ground operation and “precision” airstrikes along its border with Afghanistan, killing 29 militants in response to earlier deadly attacks.

title: Philippines Sees Slower Growth, Weak Peso Beyond Marcos’ Term
description: The Philippines has cut its economic growth targets and sees a weaker peso beyond the end of the term of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. in 2028 amid headwinds such as the Middle East tensions and an intense El Niño weather event.

title: Gold Declines as Fresh US-Iran Tension Fans Inflation Concerns
description: Gold declined to near $4,000 an ounce after the US and Iran traded attacks in the Persian Gulf, straining a ceasefire that had last week seen energy prices fall to pre-war levels and tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike.

title: Kent on Reserve Bank of Australia's New Framework
description: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools. In a speech in Sydney, Kent, who oversees financial markets at the Reserve Bank, said the cash rate target remains the primary and preferred instrument, but additional tools can play an important role during extraordinary times and provide some extra support. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
description: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China: Tech Growth Distorts Energy Demand Forecasts
description: China faces greater uncertainty in forecasting energy demand as structural changes in the economy and the rapid expansion of new industries reshape consumption patterns, according to a top government official. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AOC calls to 'break up' big tech companies amid price hikes
description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) said that large technology companies want to have "totally unchecked power" and should be split up. “The problem that we have is that these big companies, they think they are governments, they want to be governments," she said in an interview with Fox News posted Sunday, discussing tech companies and artificial...

title: Pakistan launches deadly strikes along Afghan border
description: Pakistan says its strikes were a response to "recent terrorist attacks against innocent people".

title: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
description: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

title: Anger directed at Venezuela's official response to earthquake recovery
description: Residents in quake-affected areas have expressed anger over the state's efforts to locate survivors and help victims, the BBC's Orla Guerin reports.

title: The Cost of America Abandoning the Military Draft
description: Trump could be confident his war in Iran would not touch the daily lives of most voters.

title: Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening
description: Brent crude edges up as tit-for-tat strikes imperial return to normality in key waterway.

title: Australian man charged with murder after Thai girl’s body found in suitcase
description: Simon Peter Carman has been charged with murder after the body of 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was found in a suitcase

title: Venezuelans angry with government as time runs out to rescue survivors
description: Residents and volunteers say they feel abandoned by the government as they race to save lives from the rubble.

title: Putin says Ukraine proposed halt to deep strikes
description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says Ukraine has proposed a mutual halt to long-range strikes

title: Venezuela’s earthquakes pose first major test for President Delcy Rodriguez
description: The seismic disaster has stirred public anger against government mismanagement under Venezuela's ruling socialist party.

title: Late goal gives Canada first-ever World Cup knockout stage victory
description: Canada beat South Africa 1-0 to reach the World Cup last ​16 for the first time in their history.

title: Praise pours in for ‘true Canadian heroes’ after last-minute World Cup win
description: From the prime minister to sport celebrities and fans on social media, Canadians have revelled in their team's win.

title: Pakistan says its security forces killed 29 fighters along Afghan border
description: Strikes come a day after fighters armed with guns and explosives killed three soldiers in Karachi.

title: Father and son pulled out alive four days after Venezuela earthquake
description: ootage shows search and rescue workers pulling out father and son from under the rubble of a collapsed building.

title: Who is Stephen Eustaquio, who scored for Canada against South Africa?
description: Eustaquio flip-flopped between his home country and that of his parents throughout his youth and senior career.

title: ‘If I am to die, let it be here’: Malawians fleeing unrest in South Africa
description: A mass return of Malawians from South Africa exposes the cost of migration, violence and broken livelihoods.

title: Iran war live: Tehran insists on control of Hormuz amid reports of US talks
description: Washington and Tehran have agreed to stop attacks and renew talks in the Qatari capital, Doha, Axios reports.

title: China’s first AI-powered cancer vaccine production line set to launch in Beijing
description: China has broken ground on what developers say is the country’s first production line for AI-assisted personalised tumour vaccines, raising hopes for millions of new cancer patients every year – a disease that ranks as the nation’s second-leading cause of death. By October, Beijing-based Likang Life Sciences is expected to complete a new drug research and manufacturing centre in the Beijing Economic and Technological Development Zone, with a total investment of about 110 million yuan (US$16.1...

title: Beijing’s handover anniversary policy gift to Hong Kong? It could be a ‘Reit Connect’
description: Hong Kong is seizing every opportunity to consolidate its role as a global financial centre, leveraging unique advantages and national strategies. In the third piece of our miniseries focusing on the city’s financial industry ahead of the handover anniversary, we examine the likelihood of attainable measures, such as a “Reit Connect”, being granted. As Hong Kong marks the 29th anniversary of the handover, the city’s finance sector is again watching closely for policy “gifts” from Beijing to...

title: Why brokers believe China’s AI-driven stock run will find fresh legs
description: Chinese stocks may extend their gains in the second half of the year as resilient exports and recovering producer prices support economic growth while disruption from high oil prices fades, according to brokerages at home and abroad. Average full-year earnings growth for mainland-listed companies could accelerate to 10 per cent on the strength of the economy, while households could channel 2 trillion yuan (US$294 billion) into equities, according to Guosen Securities. Founder Securities made a...

title: U.S., Iran pause hostilities as Hormuz shipping resumes after weekend clashes
description: The U.S. and Iran agreed to pause hostilities and allow commercial vessels to move freely through the Strait of Hormuz, easing fears of a prolonged disruption to global oil supplies after a weekend of military exchanges.

description: A U.S. official said the U.S. and Iran agreed to halt attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and allow vessels to move freely. Hostilities in recent days have threatened a two-week-old cease-fire.

title: Search teams rescued 33 people from the rubble on Saturday.

U.S.-Iran Deal’s Vague Language Comes Back to Haunt Peace Efforts
description: The deal called for Iran to “make arrangements” for the passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has interpreted that to mean it can designate which routes ships take.
